Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.mcp

Interface McpCursor

Functional Interface:
This is a functional interface and can therefore be used as the assignment target for a lambda expression or method reference.
@FunctionalInterface public interface McpCursor
Pagination strategy for MCP list dispatchers (tools / prompts / resources).

Built-in strategies:

  • SINGLE_PAGE - Returns everything in one page (default; ignores any incoming cursor).
  • fixedSize(int) - Slices a list into pages of a fixed size using opaque integer cursors.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final McpCursor
    SINGLE_PAGE
    Returns everything in a single page; emits no nextCursor.

    Description

    fixedSize(int pageSize)
    Returns a fixed-page-size cursor.

    Slices a descriptor list into a page returned from tools/list, prompts/list, or resources/list.
    static int
    parseOffset(String cursor)
    Parses a cursor as a non-negative integer offset; returns 0 for null or non-numeric inputs.

  • Field Details

    • SINGLE_PAGE

      static final McpCursor SINGLE_PAGE
      Returns everything in a single page; emits no nextCursor.

  • Method Details

    • page

      <T> McpPage<T> page(List<T> all, String cursor, BeanStore ctx)
      Slices a descriptor list into a page returned from tools/list, prompts/list, or resources/list.
      Type Parameters:
      T - Element type.
      Parameters:
      all - The full list of descriptors. Never null.
      cursor - Opaque cursor from the request, or null for the first page.
      ctx - Per-request bean store (provided for cursor strategies that need access to the request).
      Returns:
      A non-null page.

    • fixedSize

      static McpCursor fixedSize(int pageSize)
      Returns a fixed-page-size cursor.

      Cursor representation is an opaque integer offset (parsed leniently; non-numeric values restart at offset 0).

      Parameters:
      pageSize - Maximum items per page. Must be > 0.
      Returns:
      A new cursor strategy.

    • parseOffset

      static int parseOffset(String cursor)
      Parses a cursor as a non-negative integer offset; returns 0 for null or non-numeric inputs.
      Parameters:
      cursor - Cursor value (may be null).
      Returns:
      Parsed offset.
